model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Profile link in the dropdown menu
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=220 dy=209 conf=1.00 -> (581,414)
  iter 1: at (581,414) said dx=40 dy=-55 conf=0.90 -> (621,359)
  iter 2: at (601,386) said dx=0 dy=-34 conf=0.90 -> (601,352)
  iter 3: at (601,378) said dx=-100 dy=-18 conf=0.90 -> (501,360)
  iter 4: at (576,374) said dx=40 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (616,374)
  iter 5: at (586,374) said dx=32 dy=-21 conf=0.90 -> (618,352)
  iter 6: at (594,368) said dx=30 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (624,368)
  iter 7: at (602,368) said dx=30 dy=-27 conf=0.90 -> (632,341)
  iter 8: at (609,362) said dx=33 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (642,362)
  iter 9: at (617,362) said dx=-20 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (597,362)
  iter 10: at (612,362) said dx=-90 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (522,362)
  iter 11: at (590,362) said dx=40 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (630,362)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click on the "Profile" link in the dropdown menu.
The red crosshair is currently located at approximately (586, 374) based on previous iterations and visual estimation, but looking at the current image, it's right on the "Profile" icon.
Wait, let me look at the image carefully.
The red crosshair is centered on the user icon to the left of the word "Profile".
The "Profile" link as a whole includes the icon and the text.
The center of the "Profile" menu item would be roughly halfway between the icon and the end of the word "Profile".
The current crosshair is at the icon.
Target: "Profile" link.
The crosshair is at the icon. I should move it slightly to the right to center it on the whole "Profile" button area.
Current position: let's say (575, 375).
The word "Profile" is to the right of the icon.
Looking at the image, the crosshair is around x=575, y=375.
The center of the "Profile" item (icon + text) is probably around x=600, y=375.
Actually, looking closer at the red crosshair in the image, it's exactly on the icon.
The "Profile" text starts around x=590 and ends around x=640.
The icon is around x=570.
So the center of the "Profile" element is roughly at (570+640)/2 = 605.
Current x is ~575. dx = 605 - 575 = 30.
Current y is ~375. Target y is also ~375. dy = 0.
Wait, looking at the image again, the crosshair is very close.
Let's refine:
Icon center: ~572, 374
Text "Profile" center: ~615, 374
Full item center: ~593, 374
Current crosshair: ~572, 374
dx = 593 - 572 = 21
dy = 0
Confidence: High, but not perfect. Let's say 0.9.

Actually, looking at the previous iteration 11, the crosshair was at (590, 362) and it moved by dx=40, dy=0 to (630, 362).
Now it's at (630, 362)? No, the image shows it back on the icon.
Wait, the "Previous iterations" list is just a log.
Let's look at the *current* image.
The red crosshair is at the user icon.
Coordinates of crosshair:
The blue circle is at (599, 361) as per the bottom right label.
The red crosshair is slightly to the left and slightly below the center of the blue circle.
Blue circle center: (599, 361)
Crosshair center: looks to be around (572, 374)
Target "Profile" center: ~ (595, 374)
dx = 595 - 572 = 23
dy = 374 - 374 = 0
Let's re-evaluate the target center.
The "Profile" button area spans from roughly x=480 to x=720 (the width of the inner blue box).
Wait, the menu is centered.
The inner blue box edges: left ~ 475, right ~ 725. Center = 600.
The "Profile" text is roughly centered within this box.
The center of the "Profile" item is around (600, 374).
Current crosshair is at (~572, 374).
dx = 600 - 572 = 28.
dy = 0.
Confidence: 0.9.
